ENGLISH MOCK-EXAM

EXAMEN BLANC/ Epreuve d’Anglais

Text: Teenage pregnancy

“I was fifteen and pregnant” , said Anne , “I did not know what to do, to abort or to put the

baby up for adoption or what”. While in a few tragic cases a girl becomes pregnant because of

rape, teenage pregnancy is usually the result of voluntary participation in premarital sex. An

unmarried pregnant girl faces several painful choices. Should she get married? Should she put

the child up for adoption? Is abortion the answer?

Normally, it takes two people to make a baby, and by all rights the father of the child

should carry his full responsibility. Very often in such a situation, it’s the girl who is let to

make these difficult choices. What should the girl do? Should she get married? Many might

feel that marrying the father of the child would be the perfect solution. After all, it would save

the girl and her family from public embarrassment and it would allow the child to be raised by

two parents. But the solution is not through marriage, for rushing into marrying can’t make

the girl’s problems easy to solve since marriage is a serious step to be carefully planned. In

addition to this, the boy and the girl are still in their teens and may simply not have the

necessary emotional maturity needed to live a marriage life together.

After fully considering the matter, it might be agreed that marriage would be unwise,

and that the girl had better raise the child with the help of her family. Abortion is considered

to be a crime. So it is better to give up the child for adoption rather than ending his life by

abortion. Driven by fear, some boys who have fathered a child out of marriage fail to accept

the responsibility. A boy said, “ I just told her to look somewhere else for the father of her

baby, but some boys try to look after the child by giving financial support, others participate

in the day- to- day care of the infant. All these sometimes fail because some of the boys often

have a limited financial income. Some also lack patience and the skills to meet the demands

of a small infant. Moreover, the girl’s parents sometimes oppose to this help fearing that

continuous contact with the boy might result in further sexual contact and premature

marriage.

VOCABULARY

Teenage : adolescence

Embarrassment : une gène

To fear : to be afraid (of) : avoir peur (de)

To lack : manquer de

Rape : viol

2

I. COMPRENHENSION

A. Choose the letter corresponding to the correct answer

1. In the text, the solution to save teenage pregnancy is….

a) To make the girl kill the baby before the birth

b) To have the girl to be married

c) To make the girl be adopted

d) To help the girl raise the baby

2. Pregnancy is when a woman…

a) Gives birth to a baby

b) Expects a baby

c) Aborts by fully considering the matter

d) Adopts a baby after its birth

3. Abortion is the fact of…

a) Adopting a baby

b) Destroying a pregnancy

c) Killing a baby after the birth

d) Killing a girl for voluntary participation in premarital sex

4. Teenagers usually…

a) Have enough means to take care of the baby

b) Are mature enough to meet the child’s needs

c) Lack enough means to take care of the baby

d) Have enough patience and skills to meet the demands of a small infant

1

2

3

4

pregnancy…………….

3. Abortion is considered to be a crime…………………………….

4. Teenagers have the means to look after their children…………………………..
